title: anaphalis margaritacea var. angustior
intro: Anaphalis margaritacea var. angustior is a lesser-known variety of the Arctic or White Rabbits' Breath, a flowering plant native to high-altitude mountainous regions, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and narrow leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Anaphalis margaritacea var. angustior include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, this plant can be used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
